task_completion.description: Create a horizon line at y=4 and two vanishing points.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A horizontal line is drawn across the GeoGebra graphics view intersecting the y-axis at exactly 4. Two distinct points (vanishing points) are placed on this horizon line.

task_completion.description: Establish the outer boundaries of the perspective grid.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Lines are drawn from a starting point (usually below the horizon) converging to both the left and right vanishing points, forming the outer edges of the perspective plane.

task_completion.description: Use a mathematically accurate method for depth spacing (e.g., Diagonal Method).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Construction lines (such as diagonals crossing the perspective squares) are visible and converge to a specific point on the horizon line, proving the depth spacing is calculated geometrically rather than estimated.

task_completion.description: Complete the geometrically correct 4x4 checkerboard grid.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A complete 4x4 grid is visible, consisting of at least 10 lines (5 lines converging to the left vanishing point and 5 lines converging to the right vanishing point), creating 16 perspective squares.

A 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog is visible showing the filename 'perspective_grid.ggb' and the directory path '~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/', or the GeoGebra window title updates to 'perspective_grid.ggb'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GeoGebra GUI to construct the grid.
integrity.visual_evidence: The GeoGebra application interface (toolbar, algebra view, graphics view) is clearly visible, rather than a terminal or text editor writing a .ggb XML file directly.

integrity.description: The agent manually constructed the geometry rather than loading a pre-made file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Intermediate steps of the geometric construction (adding points, drawing lines, finding intersections) appear sequentially in the Algebra view and Graphics view.

integrity.description: The agent did not eyeball or estimate the grid spacing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Grid intersections are defined by geometric construction lines (like diagonals) rather than points placed arbitrarily or manually dragged to look 'roughly correct' along the perspective lines.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The horizon line must intersect the y-axis at exactly 4.0. A valid 4x4 checkerboard grid mathematically requires exactly 10 grid lines (5 horizontal-equivalent and 5 vertical-equivalent converging to the two vanishing points). The construction must feature mathematically accurate depth spacing, verifiable by the presence of diagonal construction lines that converge to a point on the horizon (Diagonal Method or Measuring Point method). The expected filename is 'perspective_grid.ggb'.
